title: Thought Record for Depression
description: Use this worksheet to identify, examine, and reframe negative automatic thoughts associated with depression.
instructions: When you notice a shift in your mood, complete each column below. Pay special attention to the belief ratings — these help you track how much the restructuring process changes your relationship with the thought.

fields.label: 1. Situation — What happened? What were you doing?
fields.placeholder: Briefly describe the event, interaction, or moment when your mood changed...

fields.label: 2. Automatic Thought — What went through your mind?
fields.placeholder: Write the exact thought or image that came to mind...

fields.label: 3. Belief in Thought — How much do you believe this thought? (0–100%)
4. Emotions — What did you feel? Rate intensity (0–100%)
5. Evidence For — What supports this thought?
fields.placeholder: List specific facts (not feelings) that support this thought...

fields.label: 6. Evidence Against — What contradicts this thought?
fields.placeholder: List facts, past experiences, or things others might say that contradict the thought...

fields.label: 7. Cognitive Distortion — Which thinking pattern applies?
8. Balanced Thought — What is a fairer, more balanced perspective?
fields.placeholder: Write a thought that accounts for both the evidence for and against...

fields.label: 9. New Belief Rating — How much do you believe the original thought now? (0–100%)

distortionsList.name: All-or-Nothing Thinking
distortionsList.description: Seeing things in black and white — if something isn't perfect, it's a total failure.

distortionsList.name: Overgeneralization
distortionsList.description: Drawing sweeping conclusions from a single event: 'This always happens to me.'

distortionsList.name: Mental Filter
distortionsList.description: Dwelling on negatives while filtering out positives entirely.

distortionsList.name: Disqualifying the Positive
distortionsList.description: Rejecting positive experiences: 'That doesn't count because...'

distortionsList.name: Jumping to Conclusions
distortionsList.description: Making negative interpretations without evidence (mind reading or fortune telling).

distortionsList.name: Magnification / Minimization
distortionsList.description: Blowing negatives out of proportion while shrinking positives.

distortionsList.name: Emotional Reasoning
distortionsList.description: Treating feelings as facts: 'I feel worthless, so I must be worthless.'

distortionsList.name: Should Statements
distortionsList.description: Rigid rules about how you or others 'should' behave, leading to guilt or frustration.

distortionsList.name: Labeling
distortionsList.description: Attaching a fixed label to yourself: 'I'm a failure' instead of 'I made a mistake.'

distortionsList.name: Personalization / Blame
distortionsList.description: Holding yourself entirely responsible for things outside your control.

distortionsList.name: Catastrophizing
distortionsList.description: Expecting the worst outcome and feeling certain it will happen.

disclaimer: This worksheet is for educational purposes only and is not a substitute for professional mental health treatment. If you are experiencing thoughts of self-harm or suicide, contact the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline by calling or texting 988.
